reasoning: The empirical base rate for 10x-spike ACLED questions is extremely low (0.002). The reference baseline (which measures 1 plus the 30-day average) is 1.000, revealing that there have effectively been no riots recently over a 360-day window. While Malawi faces scheduled general elections in September 2025, the targeted 30-day window falls months later in Jan-Feb 2026. A sudden massive spike precisely within this window that reaches 10+ riots out of a cold baseline is extremely improbable. Even if post-election violence were to cause riots, these occurring several months later at such high frequencies relative to the recent baseline represents an extreme tail risk. I am matching the lower bound probability allowed by the system schema at 0.02.
